title: Mtime staleness detection and rule file discovery
purpose: Context files may be edited mid-session, and rule files on disk need to be discovered and loaded into the RuleEngine. This brief combines mtime-based staleness detection (so the runtime picks up NORN.md changes without a file watcher) with rule directory scanning (bridge between rules on disk and the running engine). Both are filesystem operations in the same narrow domain — staleness can't be tested without discovery.
task: Add a staleness check method to the context loader that stats always-on context files and re-reads on mtime change. Integrate the check point into LoopContext. Create context/scanner.rs to scan ordered rule directories (.norn/rules/, ~/.norn/rules/, optionally .claude/rules/), parse .md files via parse_rule_file(), derive RuleId from file stem, and apply first-found-wins on ID collision. Add rules_dir() path helpers.

requirements.title: Staleness check method
requirements.spec: The context loader SHALL provide a check_staleness() method that stats both always-on context files and compares mtime against the last-seen values.
acceptance: check_staleness() stats ~/.norn/NORN.md and {cwd}/NORN.md
Returns false (no change) when neither file's mtime has changed
Returns true (changed) when either file's mtime differs from last-seen
Records the new mtime after detecting a change

requirements.title: Re-read on staleness
requirements.spec: WHEN check_staleness() detects a change, it SHALL re-read the changed file(s) and update the stored content. A subsequent call to the content method SHALL return the new content.
acceptance: After modifying a context file and calling check_staleness(), the content reflects the new file
Only the changed file is re-read (the unchanged file retains its cached content)
The mtime for the changed file is updated to the new value

requirements.title: Missing file handling
requirements.spec: IF a context file does not exist when check_staleness() is called, the stat SHALL complete without error. IF a file that previously existed is now missing, the stored content for that file SHALL be cleared.
acceptance: check_staleness() with neither file existing returns false and no error
A previously-existing file that is now deleted results in cleared content for that file
A file that appears for the first time mid-session is detected and loaded

requirements.title: LoopContext integration point
requirements.spec: LoopContext SHALL hold an optional reference to the context loader. A method SHALL be available to check staleness and trigger a rebuild of system_sections[0] when content has changed.
acceptance: LoopContext gains an optional context loader field
A method on LoopContext calls check_staleness() and rebuilds system_sections[0] if true
The method is callable at the start of each iteration (alongside evaluate_prompt_commands)

requirements.title: rules_dir path helpers
requirements.spec: norn::config::paths SHALL provide a rules_dir() function returning norn_dir()/rules/. norn-cli SHALL provide a project_rules_dir() for {cwd}/.norn/rules/.
acceptance: norn::config::paths::rules_dir() returns ~/.norn/rules/ (or NORN_HOME equivalent)
norn-cli exposes a project_rules_dir() returning {cwd}/.norn/rules/

requirements.title: Rule directory scanning
requirements.spec: context/scanner.rs SHALL scan an ordered list of rule directories. For each directory that exists, it SHALL enumerate all .md files, parse each via parse_rule_file(), and collect the successfully parsed rules.
acceptance: A directory containing rust-conventions.md and testing.md produces 2 parsed rules
A directory that does not exist is silently skipped
Non-.md files in the directory are ignored
Parse failures for individual files are logged via tracing::warn and skipped

requirements.title: Rule ID from file stem
requirements.spec: The rule ID SHALL be derived from the file stem: rust-conventions.md becomes RuleId("rust-conventions").
acceptance: rust-conventions.md produces RuleId("rust-conventions")
MY-RULE.md produces RuleId("MY-RULE")
The file extension (.md) is stripped, everything else is preserved

requirements.title: First-found-wins on ID collision
requirements.spec: WHEN the same rule ID is found in multiple directories (e.g. both project and user directories contain rust-conventions.md), the version from the earlier directory in the scan order SHALL be used. The later version SHALL be silently skipped.
acceptance: With project and user dirs both containing rust-conventions.md, only the project version is loaded
The skipped rule is logged at tracing::debug level
The returned Vec<Rule> contains exactly one rule per unique ID

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement file watchers (notify crate or similar) — mtime stat is sufficient
SHALL NOT modify the agent loop's iteration logic — only add a callable method on LoopContext
SHALL NOT implement nested NORN.md detection — that is NX-004
SHALL NOT implement Claude Code format compatibility — that is NX-003
SHALL NOT wire into build_runtime — that is NX-005
SHALL NOT modify the RuleEngine internals — only add rules via the public add_rule() method
verification: cargo test -p norn -- context passes all tests
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
